summary: Frontend-only test of Call Team / Consult balance changes. Wrote 37 deterministic logic tests against /app/frontend/src/game/{items,battle,clinical}.ts using sucrase-node. ALL 37 PASSED. Verified: AP costs (Pharmacy/Respiratory/Infection=2 AP, Rapid=3 AP); Pharmacy-without-clue adds Lab Token + no discount + correct log; Pharmacy-with-Wheezing adds Albuterol Mist + preparedItemDiscount=Albuterol Mist + effective cost 1 AP; Respiratory inappropriate on river_sludge increments inappropriateConsultsUsed + does NOT unlock open_airflow + correct log; Respiratory appropriate on Air Sprite unlocks open_airflow AND nextAirActionDiscount stays false AND Breath of Dawn consumes full AP (no discount); Rapid hard-gate aborts at stability>30 with no danger trigger and succeeds at stability≤30 with +15 stability, shieldNext=100, emergencyCallsUsed++; Infection inappropriate on Air Sprite increments inappropriateConsultsUsed + correct log + no temp action; Star penalties for consultsUsed=2, emergencyCallsUsed=1, inappropriateConsultsUsed=1 each withhold the 3rd star; One appropriate consult still earns 3 stars. Regression: dynamic AP=4 at full stability, hero-action-once-per-turn enforced (second applySkill aborts), endPlayerTurn no longer auto-restores AP after Rapid (rapidResponseActive cleared, AP recomputed dynamically). Verified Expo web preview loads without red-screen.
action_items: Note: A non-blocking expo console warning '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' was emitted on home load — unrelated to this feature, but worth a sweep later.

Full Playwright run-through of the actual Battle screen (onboarding → trial battle → Call tab) was skipped to stay within scope and tokens; logic + URL-param plumbing was confirmed directly. Main agent may self-test the result.tsx CONSULT USE card visually if desired (it renders only when consults/emergency/inappropriate > 0).
critical_code_review_comments: battle.tsx line 472: rapidGated badge text exactly matches spec ('Reserved for Stability ≤ 30').
result.tsx uses COLORS.warning with fallback to COLORS.brand for consultWarn — safe since COLORS.warning is already used elsewhere (battle.tsx stabilityColor).

applyCall: consultsUsed counter is incremented for ALL calls including Rapid Response (line 548). Per the request, Rapid uses emergencyCallsUsed; but consultsUsed also ticks up. Star logic treats Rapid as both consult+emergency, so a lone Rapid yields consultsUsed=1 + emergencyCallsUsed=1 → emergency penalty triggers correctly (verified). This is consistent with spec.

battle.tsx availableCalls filter (line 129-133) hides Respiratory if not Air/no wheez clue and Infection if not Fire — this means the INAPPROPRIATE path for Respiratory/Infection cannot normally be triggered from the UI on Air Sprite (Infection button is filtered out). However the test spec asks for INFECTION-INAPPROPRIATE on Air Sprite — UI filtering would prevent the user from ever calling it. Logic-level handling is correct, but UI filter may mask this code path. Main agent: confirm whether the filter should allow inappropriate consults to be selected so the inappropriate-penalty path is reachable through the UI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable: /app/frontend/tests/consult_balance.test.ts runs via `cd /app/frontend && npx sucrase-node tests/consult_balance.test.ts`. Covers all consult balance invariants (AP costs, pharmacy clue gating + discount, respiratory/infection inappropriate paths, rapid hard-gate, star penalties, regression for dynamic AP and hero-action-per-turn). One nuance to flag: UI filter in battle.tsx availableCalls hides call_infection from non-Fire enemies, so the user cannot trigger Infection-on-Air-Sprite through normal UI — the inappropriate-penalty code path exists in applyCall but is currently unreachable for that specific case via UI.
